message:
feat(ffs-types): add checked arithmetic + alignment helpers (bd-sik)

Centralize block math, offset arithmetic, and alignment operations with
overflow checking. Provides free functions (checked_add_bytes,
checked_mul_block, align_down, align_up, u64_to_usize, u64_to_u32) plus
methods on BlockNumber and ByteOffset. Includes 9 new unit tests covering
overflow, underflow, alignment edge cases, and narrowing conversions.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -85,7 +85,7 @@
 {"id":"bd-owq","title":"ffs-error: Define canonical error taxonomy + mapping boundaries","description":"Goal: make error handling consistent across layers:
- ffs-ondisk returns ParseError (pure parsing)
- ffs-core/ffs-fuse/ffs-cli return FfsError (user-facing)

Deliverables:
- Document the mapping from ParseError -> FfsError (which variants, and when we preserve details).
- Ensure ffs-error stays independent of ffs-ondisk (avoid cyclic deps).
- Update docs to reference the canonical listing in crates/ffs-error/src/lib.rs.

Acceptance:
- No doc section lists a different FfsError variant set than the code.
- FUSE errno mapping is complete and matches chosen policy.","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-10T03:14:37.381664627Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-10T03:14:37.381664627Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["foundation"]}
 {"id":"bd-p1l","title":"Perf: Golden outputs + isomorphism proof protocol for optimizations","description":"Goal: enforce the extreme-software-optimization loop.

Deliverables:
- Define which commands produce golden outputs (JSON reports, fixture parse summaries).
- Store sha256 checksums for goldens.
- Require an isomorphism proof note for each optimization (ordering preserved, tie-breaking unchanged, etc.).

Acceptance:
- There is a repeatable command to verify goldens before/after.
- Optimization PRs cannot "accidentally" change behavior without detection.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-10T03:30:48.769379006Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-10T03:31:10.590334115Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["harness","perf"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-p1l","depends_on_id":"bd-6st","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-10T03:31:10.590283149Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-q0g","title":"ext4 semantics: Implement permission/uid/gid + mode mapping details [phased]","description":"Future task: ensure getattr reports correct permission bits and ownership, and FUSE enforces them where appropriate.

Deliverables:
- Map inode mode bits to POSIX file types + permissions.
- Surface uid/gid correctly (including high bits).

Acceptance:
- getattr output matches kernel for a fixture inode set.","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-10T03:27:47.212225418Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-10T03:28:01.896740669Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["ext4","semantics"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-q0g","depends_on_id":"bd-10t","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-10T03:28:01.896707087Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-q0g","depends_on_id":"bd-3qq","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-10T03:28:01.815846651Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
-{"id":"bd-sik","title":"ffs-types: Checked arithmetic + alignment helpers (block math, offsets)","description":"Goal: centralize all tricky integer math used by on-disk parsing and block mapping.

Deliverables:
- checked_add_bytes(offset, len) -> ByteOffset end
- checked_mul_block(block_nr, block_size) -> ByteOffset
- align_down/align_up helpers with overflow checking
- conversions with explicit error paths (no silent truncation)

Acceptance:
- Unit tests cover overflow/edge cases.
- Call sites in ffs-block/ffs-ondisk/ffs-core prefer these helpers over ad-hoc math.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-10T03:14:24.565357285Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-10T03:14:24.565357285Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["foundation"]}

Deliverables:
- checked_add_bytes(offset, len) -> ByteOffset end

Acceptance:

- Call sites in ffs-block/ffs-ondisk/ffs-core prefer these helpers over ad-hoc math.","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"AzureBeaver","created_at":"2026-02-10T03:14:24.565357285Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-10T08:39:49.756821241Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["foundation"]}
 {"id":"bd-vgu","title":"FUSE: Add fuser dependency + skeleton Filesystem impl delegating to FsOps","description":"Goal: make ffs-fuse a thin adapter: kernel FUSE requests -> internal FsOps.

Deliverables:
- Add fuser crate dependency (phase-gated if needed).
- Implement fuser::Filesystem with stubs that call into a boxed FsOps.
- Ensure all errors map through FfsError::to_errno().

Acceptance:
- cargo test passes with fuser integrated.
- The filesystem can be mounted in a no-op mode (returns ENOSYS for unimplemented ops).","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-10T03:28:25.716873087Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-10T03:28:51.924008708Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["fuse"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-vgu","depends_on_id":"bd-2tq","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-10T03:28:51.835670432Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-vgu","depends_on_id":"bd-3is","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-10T03:28:51.923969054Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-wse","title":"ext4 semantics: Implement symlink handling (fast/slow) [phased]","description":"Future task: support symlinks.

Deliverables:
- Fast symlinks stored inline in inode i_block.
- Slow symlinks stored as file data.

Acceptance:
- readlink works in harness/FUSE on a fixture symlink.","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-10T03:26:33.602112189Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-10T03:28:01.649855179Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["ext4","semantics"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-wse","depends_on_id":"bd-10t","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-10T03:28:01.570813210Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-wse","depends_on_id":"bd-2q7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-10T03:28:01.649820965Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-ye4","title":"ext4 semantics: Implement extent mapping (ffs-extent) for regular files","description":"Goal: map file logical offsets to physical blocks using the ext4 extent tree.

Deliverables:
- ffs-extent: a function that takes an inode extent root and returns an iterator of (logical_block -> physical_start,len,unwritten?).
- Support depth>0 extent trees by reading extent blocks from disk (requires BlockDevice).
- Provide a read_extent_at(file_block) helper for file reads.

Acceptance:
- Tests cover: leaf-only extents and index+leaf extents.
- Verified on a fixture file with known extents (compare to debugfs stats or golden outputs).","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-10T03:25:46.487585491Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-10T03:27:33.455693206Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["ext4","semantics"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-ye4","depends_on_id":"bd-10t","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-10T03:27:33.350893907Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-ye4","depends_on_id":"bd-3qq","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-10T03:27:33.455657288Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
